Name: pac-bayes_latency_optimization
Title: PAC-Bayes Frameworks for Latency-Optimized Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: Leveraging PAC-Bayes frameworks can yield new insights into optimizing latency in machine learning inference, particularly by formalizing strict latency guarantees while maintaining model accuracy.
Related Work: Existing works primarily focus on either PAC-Bayes theory or various latency optimization techniques, but few explicitly integrate these approaches. For instance, while papers explore mixed precision quantization and adaptive sampling, they do not address the latency constraints from a PAC-Bayes perspective, which can impose rigorous guarantees on generalization and performance.
Abstract: This proposal investigates the integration of PAC-Bayes frameworks into the realm of latency-optimized machine learning inference. By framing the inference process within a PAC-Bayes context, we aim to derive formal guarantees for latency-sensitive applications while maintaining acceptable accuracy levels. Our approach will utilize a Jacobian-based layer-skipping technique that optimally selects which layers to evaluate during inference, depending on the current state of the model and input data characteristics. We will experimentally validate our method against existing latency optimization strategies, focusing on throughput-accuracy trade-offs and strict latency budgets. The expected outcomes include enhanced performance metrics and a deeper understanding of the trade-offs between model complexity and operational efficiency, paving the way for future work in latency-critical applications.
Experiments: Implement a baseline model with traditional inference techniques and measure its latency and accuracy.
Develop the PAC-Bayes optimized inference model utilizing the Jacobian-based layer-skipping framework.
Perform a comparative analysis on the two models using standardized datasets, employing metrics such as throughput, accuracy, and latency under various load conditions.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of different components of the PAC-Bayes framework on both latency and accuracy.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of implementing the PAC-Bayes framework in practice, which may not yield the expected improvements in all scenarios. Additionally, the reliance on theoretical guarantees might limit the flexibility in practical, real-world applications. Lastly, the computational overhead of maintaining precise latency metrics may affect overall performance.
